title: rosa communis var. acanthina
intro: Rosa communis var. acanthina, also known as the wild rose or dog rose, is a type of flowering shrub that belongs to the rose family. It is valued for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and astringent properties, making it effective in treating wounds, skin issues, and digestive problems. The health benefits of Rosa communis var. acanthina include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and supporting the immune system. The plant can be prepared into various herbal remedies, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, and can also be used in the form of rosehip oil, which is rich in antioxidants and essential fatty acids.
